topic.name: Climate Change Mitigation
topic.description: Strategies and actions aimed at reducing the impacts of climate change through various means.

doc1: The importance of reducing carbon emissions.

topic.name: sustainable energy solutions
topic.description: The documents discuss various approaches and technologies aimed at promoting sustainable energy practices.

doc2: impact of wind energy on reducing carbon footprint
doc3: biomass as a sustainable fuel alternative
government policies supporting green energy initiatives

topic.name: Sustainable Urban Development
topic.description: The documents discuss strategies and practices for creating environmentally sustainable cities.

doc3: water conservation techniques in urban areas

topic.name: Medicare Legislation and Reforms
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative reforms related to Medicare, specifically addressing participant rights, drug pricing, and access to prescription drug benefits.

Document 1: Medicare Advantage Participant Bill of Rights Act of 2014
requires a Medicare Advantage organization to remove a service provider or a supplier from a plan network only for cause
Document 2: Medicare Drug Savings Act of 2011
requires drug manufacturers to pay the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) drug rebates for rebate eligible individuals
Document 3: Medicare Prescription Drug Savings for Our Seniors Act of 2007
direct the Secretary of Health and Human Services to offer one or more Medicare operated prescription drug plans

topic.name: Marine Renewable Energy and Tax Incentives
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation related to the promotion of marine renewable energy technologies and associated tax incentives for charitable contributions and conservation efforts.

Document 1: Requires the Department of Energy (DOE) to establish a program of marine renewable energy research
developing new marine renewable energy technologies
Document 2: Suspends limitations on the tax deduction for charitable contributions made by individuals and corporations
Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2010 the tax deduction for charitable contributions of food inventory
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to make permanent the tax deduction for charitable contributions by individuals and corporations of real property interests for conservation purposes

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative changes to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States regarding temporary duty suspensions and reductions on specific goods.

Document 1: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on calcium chloride phosphate.
Document 2: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to reduce temporarily the duty on golf wedge club heads.
Document 3: Amends the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States to extend the temporary suspension of duty on Phosphoric acid, tris (2-ethylhexyl) ester.

topic.name: Intelligence and Defense Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses U.S. legislative measures related to intelligence operations, national security, and defense resource management.

Document 1: Prohibits the Attorney General... from initiating a custodial interrogation... against any foreign person detained by the United States
Requires an annual report from the AG to Congress
Document 2: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011 for the conduct of intelligence and intelligence-related activities

Prohibits the authorization of appropriations by this Act from being deemed to constitute authority to conduct any intelligence activity not otherwise authorized by the Constitution or laws of the United States
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Defense... to develop and promulgate guidelines for the Department of Defense
Requires the Secretary to report annually to Congress on Army arsenal capabilities, workload, performance, and required capital investments

topic.name: Health and Land Management
topic.description: The documents address the management and transfer of land for health-related purposes and the improvement of mental health infrastructure.

Document 1: convey specified property, including all land appurtenances, in Kotzebue, Alaska, to the Maniilaq Association for use in connection with health and social services programs
Shields the Maniilaq Association from liability for soil, surface water, groundwater, or other contamination
Document 2: authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services to award grants to eligible entities for the construction or modernization of facilities to provide mental health and behavioral health services
assurances that facilities will be used for not less than 10 years for community-based mental health or substance abuse services

topic.name: Veterans Affairs and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at supporting veterans through employment, legal protections, and healthcare services.

Document 1: establish a veteran conservation corps
assist veterans in their transition from military service to civilian life
Document 2: subject to punishment by a court-martial any person who, with the intent to retaliate against any individual for reporting a criminal offense
DOD personnel who investigate claims of retaliation in connection with reports of sexual assault in the Armed Forces shall receive training
Document 3: designates the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) medical center in Spokane, Washington

topic.name: Immigration Enforcement Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing immigration enforcement and the management of criminal aliens in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Illegal Immigration Reform and Immigrant Responsibility Act of 1996
revised deadline and funding provisions for fencing and security project requirements along the southern border.
Document 2: directs the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) to establish a law enforcement notification system
notify DHS of the release from custody of an alien who has been arrested for certain offenses
Document 3: Prohibits a state or political subdivision from receiving funds under any program or activity administered by DHS
compiles statistics on each person who is arrested, charged with a crime, convicted, or incarcerated after being convicted of a crime by the state

topic.name: Consumer Product Safety Regulations
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring the safety of consumer products through testing, labeling, and restrictions on harmful substances.

Document 1: Genetically Engineered Salmon Risk Reduction Act
requires food that contains genetically engineered salmon to bear a label stating that fact.
Document 2: Prohibits manufacturers, distributors, or retailers from selling, manufacturing for sale, distributing in commerce, or importing into the United States any children's product or upholstered furniture that contains a flame retardant exceeding a specified amount
requires the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) to appoint a chronic hazard advisory panel to study the effects on human health of all chemical flame retardants.
Document 3: Amends the Consumer Product Safety Act to require consumer product testing programs
Requires nongovernmental, independent third party testing of children's products.

topic.name: Transportation Security and Infrastructure
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing security measures and improving infrastructure within the transportation sector, including air and rail systems.

Document 1: Airport Security Enhancement Act of 2007 - Amends passenger and property screening provisions to require the screening by Transportation Security Administration (TSA) personnel.
Requires the heads of specified hub airports and nonhub airports to submit to the Administrator of TSA a plan for comprehensive screening.
Document 2: Passenger Train Derailment Prevention Act requires each railroad carrier to submit to the Department of Transportation (DOT) a list identifying each curve, bridge, tunnel, or other location where the maximum authorized operating speed is at least 20 miles per hour less than the maximum approach speed.
DOT shall exercise its emergency authority to require additional safety measures for passenger trains.
Document 3: National Infrastructure Bank Act of 2011 establishes an independent National Infrastructure Bank to designate qualified transit, public housing, water, highway, bridge, aviation, freight, passenger rail, or road infrastructure projects for loans.
Issue general purpose and project-based infrastructure bonds exempt from state and local taxation.

topic.name: Employment and Wage Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on unemployment benefits and gender pay equity in the workplace.

Document 1: expands exceptions to the prohibition against sex discrimination
strategies to increase the participation of women in high-wage, high-demand occupations
Document 2: extend emergency unemployment compensation payments for eligible individuals
federal payments to states cover 100% of EUC
Document 3: extend emergency unemployment compensation payments for eligible individuals
expresses the sense of Congress that the House of Representatives and the Senate should reconvene

topic.name: Federal Legislation and Designations
topic.description: This topic encompasses federal laws and designations related to buildings, land use, and environmental protection.

Document 1: Designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 100 East 8th Avenue in Pine Bluff, Arkansas, as the 'George Howard, Jr. Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
Document 2: Amends federal law authorizing Rockingham County, Virginia, to use specified land transferred by the United States for a child care center to remove that use restriction.
Document 3: Designates the New York/New Jersey Bight as the New Jersey/New York Clean Ocean Zone.
Prohibits the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, the Secretary of the Army, and all states from issuing a permit for ocean dumping.

topic.name: Government Reporting and Regulation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at modifying or eliminating reporting requirements and regulations across various federal agencies and sectors.

Document 1: elimination or modification of reporting and notification requirements of specified federal agencies
eliminates peanut base acres data collection and publication under the Food, Conservation, and Energy Act of 2008
Document 2: postpones the effective date of final rule of the Department of Labor revising income thresholds for determining overtime pay
Document 3: establish a hospital residency loan program that provides loans to eligible public or nonprofit hospitals
requires the Administrator to establish penalties for hospitals that violate provisions of this Act

topic.name: USPS Innovation and Delivery Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing the United States Postal Service's innovation capabilities and delivery methods.

Document 1: Establishes in the U.S. Postal Service (USPS) the position of Chief Innovation Officer
Requires the Postmaster General... to submit to Congress a comprehensive strategy for maximizing revenues through innovative postal and nonpostal products and services.
Document 2: Declares that it is the policy of the United States Postal Service (USPS) to provide access to secure, convenient package delivery receptacles
Directs USPS to provide a primary mode of mail delivery other than door delivery, with a preference for secure, centralized delivery.

topic.name: Election and Voter Registration Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on improving election processes, voter registration, and transparency in voting.

Document 1: Amends the Help America Vote Act of 2002 to require states to meet specified standards for transparent and accurate tabulation of votes
Requires the Election Assistance Commission to reimburse states to cover the costs of carrying out tabulations
Document 2: Requires jurisdictions to establish and maintain a jurisdiction-wide arsonist registry
Prohibits public access to such information
Document 3: Amends UOCAVA to require states to establish procedures for absentee uniform services voters
Requires the chief state election official to develop a free access tracking system for absentee ballots

topic.name: Abortion Legislation and Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legal measures and regulations surrounding abortion practices, healthcare provider rights, and the protection of newborns resulting from abortion procedures.

Document 1: Subjects any physician who knowingly performs an abortion in violation of this Act to fines and/or imprisonment.
Requires a physician who performs an abortion to have admitting privileges at a local hospital.
Document 2: Prohibition against the federal government and state and local governments penalizing or discriminating against a health care provider based on the provider's refusal to be involved in, or provide coverage for, abortion.
The Office for Civil Rights must investigate complaints alleging discrimination based on an individual's religious belief, moral conviction, or refusal to be involved in an abortion.
Document 3: Requires any health care practitioner who is present when a child is born alive following an abortion to exercise the same degree of care as reasonably provided to any other child born alive.
An individual who commits an overt act that kills a child born alive is subject to criminal prosecution for murder.

topic.name: U.S. Policy and Legislative Frameworks
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts aimed at addressing critical issues such as economic sustainability, global hunger, and healthcare liability in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Securing America's Future Economy (SAFE) Commission to develop legislation designed to address the unsustainable imbalance between long-term federal spending commitments and projected revenues.
Requires CBO to prepare a long-term cost estimate and have it published in the Congressional Record as expeditiously as possible whenever requested.
Document 2: Declares that it shall be U.S. policy to reduce global hunger, advance nutrition, increase food security, and ensure that relevant federal policies and programs provide emergency response and direct support to vulnerable populations.
Establishes in the Executive Office of the President a White House Office on Global Hunger and Food Security.
Document 3: Establishes a framework for health care liability lawsuits to undergo review by independent medical review panels if health care professionals allege adherence to applicable clinical practice guidelines.
Requires the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) to publish clinical practice guidelines provided and maintained by national or state medical societies.

topic.name: Legislative Amendments and Programs
topic.description: This cluster discusses various legislative amendments aimed at improving compensation systems, defense procurement processes, and early childhood education funding.

Document 1: Amends the Longshore and Harbor Workers' Compensation Act
specifies requirements for proportional payment of compensation
Document 2: Amends the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2010
authority of the Secretary of the Navy to enter into multiyear contracts
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Budget Reconciliation Act of 1981
establish a Prekindergarten Incentive Fund

topic.name: Financial Regulation and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at providing regulatory relief and support to small financial institutions and promoting energy storage initiatives.

Document 3: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to exclude from regulation as a swap dealer an insured depository institution or a U.S. uninsured branch or agency of a foreign bank that has a prudential regulator.

Excludes outright from the meaning of financial entity any small bank, savings association, farm credit system institution, or credit union if the aggregate uncollateralized outward exposure does not exceed $1 billion.

topic.name: Employee Privacy Protection Laws
topic.description: Legislation aimed at safeguarding employee privacy by prohibiting employers from accessing personal online accounts and requiring password disclosure.

Document 1: subject to a fine any employer who knowingly and intentionally compels or coerces any person to provide the employer with a password
discharges, disciplines, discriminates, or threatens to take such actions against any person who fails to authorize access
Document 3: Prohibits employers from requiring or requesting that an employee or applicant for employment provide a user name, password
discharging, disciplining, discriminating against, denying employment or promotion to, or threatening to take any such action against any employee or applicant who refuses to provide such information

topic.name: U.S. Legislative Acts
topic.description: The documents discuss various legislative acts related to education funding, military construction, and international financing regulations.

Document 1: Keep Our Promise to America's Children and Teachers Act
appropriations for school improvement programs
Document 2: Military Construction Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2009
authorizes the Secretary of the Army to acquire real property
Document 3: Hizballah International Financing Prevention Act of 2015
directs the President to report to Congress annually identifying providers that have knowingly entered into a contractual relationship with al-Manar TV

topic.name: Marine Conservation Legislation
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative measures aimed at protecting marine ecosystems and species, particularly addressing harmful algal blooms and whale conservation.

Document 1: Amends the Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Research and Control Act of 1998
establish and maintain a National Harmful Algal Bloom and Hypoxia Program
Document 2: Amends the Whale Conservation and Protection Study Act
restates strong U.S. opposition to commercial whaling

topic.name: Recreational Land and Water Management
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the management and enhancement of recreational access and safety in land and water resources.

Document 1: amends the Water Resources Development Act of 1986 to authorize the Department of the Army to make modifications to deep-draft, high-commercial-use channels
ensure safe navigation and efficient operations of such channels
Document 2: revises a variety of existing programs to expand access to, and opportunities for, hunting, fishing, and recreational shooting
increasing the proportion of funding from the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act that states may use for public target ranges
Document 3: designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management in Humboldt County, Nevada, as wilderness
authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary measures in such wilderness to control fire, insects, and diseases
